game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **ウボンゴ – ダス・ワイルド・レゲスピエル (Ubongo):** Players are presented with a grid-based puzzle board and 3-4 unique polyomino pieces, with the core mechanic being to drag and drop these pieces using touch to perfectly fill the designated area without overlap or gaps. Tapping a piece rotates it 90 degrees clockwise, while a two-finger pinch gesture flips it, allowing precise manipulation. In "Level Mode," players progress through hundreds of increasingly difficult puzzles, earning in-game "gems" based on completion speed, which can unlock hints or new puzzle sets. "Free Mode" offers over 6,000 randomly generated puzzles, and "Time Attack" challenges players to solve as many as possible within a 60-second limit. Multiplayer mode allows up to 4 players to simultaneously solve individual puzzles, with the fastest correct solver earning the most gems.
game_info.description: Welcome to Ubongo Puzzle Challenge! Your goal is to fill the highlighted puzzle board area completely using the provided polyomino pieces. Each piece must fit perfectly without overlaps or gaps. Complete puzzles quickly to earn more gems and unlock new challenges. Progress through increasingly difficult levels and test your spatial reasoning skills!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move selected piece on the board
Space: Rotate selected piece 90° clockwise
Shift: Flip selected piece horizontally
Z: Cycle through available pieces (select next piece)
Enter: Start the game
Esc: Pause/Unpause the game
R: Restart and return to start screen

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and piece manipulation test. This test validates that pieces can be moved, rotated, and placed on the board correctly. It tests the core mechanics of piece selection, movement, and basic placement.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Select the first piece using Z, move it to various positions using arrow keys, rotate it using Space, and test flip functionality with Shift. Verify that pieces move correctly and respond to all control inputs without errors.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The test is successful if the player piece responds to all inputs correctly, moves within board boundaries, rotates and flips as expected, and the game state updates appropriately. No errors should occur during piece manipulation.

TEST_2.test_description: Win condition test. This test implements an optimal strategy to solve puzzles and reach the GAME_OVER_WIN state. It validates that the puzzle can be completed and the win condition is properly detected.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Analyze the puzzle board and available pieces, calculate optimal placement positions and orientations for each piece, systematically place pieces to fill the board completely, and verify the puzzle solution is accepted as complete.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The test is successful when all pieces are correctly placed on the board filling the target area completely, the game detects the win condition, transitions to GAME_OVER_WIN state, and displays the victory screen with earned gems.
